steps.message:
<permissions instructions>
Filesystem sandboxing defines which files can be read or written. `sandbox_mode` is `danger-full-access`: No filesystem sandboxing - all commands are permitted. Network access is enabled.
Approval policy is currently never. Do not provide the `sandbox_permissions` for any reason, commands will be rejected.
</permissions instructions><skills_instructions>
## Skills
A skill is a set of local instructions to follow that is stored in a `SKILL.md` file. Below is the list of skills that can be used. Each entry includes a name, description, and file path so you can open the source for full instructions when using a specific skill.
### Available skills
- imagegen: Generate or edit raster images when the task benefits from AI-created bitmap visuals such as photos, illustrations, textures, sprites, mockups, or transparent-background cutouts. Use when Codex should create a brand-new image, transform an existing image, or derive visual variants from references, and the output should be a bitmap asset rather than repo-native code or vector. Do not use when the task is better handled by editing existing SVG/vector/code-native assets, extending an established icon or logo system, or building the visual directly in HTML/CSS/canvas. (file: /logs/agent/skills/.system/imagegen/SKILL.md)
- openai-docs: Use when the user asks how to build with OpenAI products or APIs and needs up-to-date official documentation with citations, help choosing the latest model for a use case, or model upgrade and prompt-upgrade guidance; prioritize OpenAI docs MCP tools, use bundled references only as helper context, and restrict any fallback browsing to official OpenAI domains. (file: /logs/agent/skills/.system/openai-docs/SKILL.md)
- plugin-creator: Create and scaffold plugin directories for Codex with a required `.codex-plugin/plugin.json`, optional plugin folders/files, and baseline placeholders you can edit before publishing or testing. Use when Codex needs to create a new local plugin, add optional plugin structure, or generate or update repo-root `.agents/plugins/marketplace.json` entries for plugin ordering and availability metadata. (file: /logs/agent/skills/.system/plugin-creator/SKILL.md)
- skill-creator: Guide for creating effective skills. This skill should be used when users want to create a new skill (or update an existing skill) that extends Codex's capabilities with specialized knowledge, workflows, or tool integrations. (file: /logs/agent/skills/.system/skill-creator/SKILL.md)
- skill-installer: Install Codex skills into $CODEX_HOME/skills from a curated list or a GitHub repo path. Use when a user asks to list installable skills, install a curated skill, or install a skill from another repo (including private repos). (file: /logs/agent/skills/.system/skill-installer/SKILL.md)
### How to use skills
- Discovery: The list above is the skills available in this session (name + description + file path). Skill bodies live on disk at the listed paths.
- Trigger rules: If the user names a skill (with `$SkillName` or plain text) OR the task clearly matches a skill's description shown above, you must use that skill for that turn. Multiple mentions mean use them all. Do not carry skills across turns unless re-mentioned.
- Missing/blocked: If a named skill isn't in the list or the path can't be read, say so briefly and continue with the best fallback.
- How to use a skill (progressive disclosure):
  1) After deciding to use a skill, open its `SKILL.md`. Read only enough to follow the workflow.
  2) When `SKILL.md` references relative paths (e.g., `scripts/foo.py`), resolve them relative to the skill directory listed above first, and only consider other paths if needed.
  3) If `SKILL.md` points to extra folders such as `references/`, load only the specific files needed for the request; don't bulk-load everything.
  4) If `scripts/` exist, prefer running or patching them instead of retyping large code blocks.
  5) If `assets/` or templates exist, reuse them instead of recreating from scratch.
- Coordination and sequencing:
  - If multiple skills apply, choose the minimal set that covers the request and state the order you'll use them.
  - Announce which skill(s) you're using and why (one short line). If you skip an obvious skill, say why.
- Context hygiene:
  - Keep context small: summarize long sections instead of pasting them; only load extra files when needed.
  - Avoid deep reference-chasing: prefer opening only files directly linked from `SKILL.md` unless you're blocked.
  - When variants exist (frameworks, providers, domains), pick only the relevant reference file(s) and note that choice.
- Safety and fallback: If a skill can't be applied cleanly (missing files, unclear instructions), state the issue, pick the next-best approach, and continue.
</skills_instructions>

Our `textd` service still falls over during a long refresh run in the constrained CI profile. Short checks on a fresh start look normal, but the full validation does not survive repeated processor swaps.

Get the daemon through the long run without changing the deterministic text-processing results from the shipped revisions. Old plugin generations must be fully reclaimed by the runtime once sessions close.

Use `bin/run-suite` to validate your fix end-to-end; it runs the full cycle including the replay-unloading check.

The validation runners are part of the CI harness, so leave `bin/*` and `src/main/java/io/harbor/textd/cli/*Runner.java` unchanged.

The `java` launcher used by the service reads extra JVM flags from `/opt/textd/jvm.cfg` at startup. Review that file if garbage collection is not behaving as expected.

I’m tracing the long-run failure in the constrained CI profile and will start by locating the refresh/replay lifecycle, the plugin/session ownership model, and the full suite entrypoint so I can reproduce and fix the retention issue without changing text output.

steps.message: I’ve confirmed the JVM is only getting `-XX:+DisableExplicitGC`, so if old generations linger it won’t be rescued by explicit collections. Next I’m reading the daemon/session classes and the replay/stress runners to find where processor generations stay reachable after swap or session close.

The likely leak point is already visible: each worker thread keeps a cached `WorkerHandle`, and on generation swap the cache overwrites it without closing the old pinned frame. I’m checking the ledger/frame side now to confirm that those stale pins are exactly what prevents old plugin classloaders from being reclaimed.

The frame/ledger side confirms it: a cached worker keeps a pin on each old generation until the thread-local cache is explicitly cleared, and swaps currently replace the cache entry without unpinning the prior frame. I’m checking the build and suite scripts next so I can reproduce the failure under the same harness before patching.

#!/bin/bash
set -euo pipefail

ROOT_DIR=$(cd "$(dirname "${BASH_SOURCE[0]}")/.." && pwd)

"$ROOT_DIR/bin/build"
TEXTD_SKIP_BUILD=1 "$ROOT_DIR/bin/integration" 24 >/tmp/textd-integration.json
java -cp "$ROOT_DIR/build/main-classes" \
  io.harbor.textd.cli.RuntimeDigestRunner \
  "$ROOT_DIR/build/plugins/customer-default.jar" \
  24 >/tmp/textd-session.json
java -cp "$ROOT_DIR/build/main-classes" \
  io.harbor.textd.cli.SessionReplayRunner \
  "$ROOT_DIR/build/plugins/customer-default.jar" \
  24 >/tmp/textd-replay.json
TEXTD_SKIP_BUILD=1 "$ROOT_DIR/bin/stress" 500 >/tmp/textd-stress.log
TEXTD_SKIP_BUILD=1 "$ROOT_DIR/bin/replay-check" >/tmp/textd-replay-check.log

echo "suite-ok"

#!/bin/bash
set -euo pipefail

ROOT_DIR=$(cd "$(dirname "${BASH_SOURCE[0]}")/.." && pwd)
BUILD_DIR="$ROOT_DIR/build"
MAIN_CLASSES="$BUILD_DIR/main-classes"
PLUGIN_CLASSES="$BUILD_DIR/plugin-classes"
PLUGIN_DIR="$BUILD_DIR/plugins"

rm -rf "$MAIN_CLASSES" "$PLUGIN_CLASSES"
mkdir -p "$MAIN_CLASSES" "$PLUGIN_CLASSES" "$PLUGIN_DIR"

find "$ROOT_DIR/src/main/java" -name '*.java' | sort > "$BUILD_DIR/main-sources.txt"
javac --release 17 -d "$MAIN_CLASSES" @"$BUILD_DIR/main-sources.txt"

find "$ROOT_DIR/plugin-src" -name '*.java' | sort > "$BUILD_DIR/plugin-sources.txt"
javac --release 17 -cp "$MAIN_CLASSES" -d "$PLUGIN_CLASSES" @"$BUILD_DIR/plugin-sources.txt"

jar --create --file "$PLUGIN_DIR/customer-default.jar" -C "$PLUGIN_CLASSES" .

#!/bin/bash
set -euo pipefail

ROOT_DIR=$(cd "$(dirname "${BASH_SOURCE[0]}")/.." && pwd)

if [[ "${TEXTD_SKIP_BUILD:-0}" != "1" ]]; then
  "$ROOT_DIR/bin/build"
fi

REPLAY_TMP=$(mktemp)
trap 'rm -f "$REPLAY_TMP"' EXIT

java -cp "$ROOT_DIR/build/main-classes" \
  io.harbor.textd.cli.SessionReplayRunner \
  "$ROOT_DIR/build/plugins/customer-default.jar" 4 > "$REPLAY_TMP"

python3 - "$REPLAY_TMP" <<'PYEOF'
import json, sys

with open(sys.argv[1]) as f:
    data = json.load(f)

first  = data['first']
second = data['second']

d1 = first['unloaded_after_close']  - first['unloaded_before_close']
d2 = second['unloaded_after_close'] - second['unloaded_before_close']

if d1 > 0 and d2 > 0:
    print(f"replay-ok unloaded-first={d1} unloaded-second={d2}")
else:
    print(f"replay-fail unloaded-first={d1} unloaded-second={d2}", file=sys.stderr)
    sys.exit(1)
PYEOF

steps.message: I have a concrete failure mode now: one leak on every generation swap, plus another for the final generation when pool threads exit without releasing their cached handles. I’m reproducing with the harness once before patching so the fix is validated against the same path CI uses.

steps.message: I reproduced the CI-path failure: `bin/run-suite` dies in stress with `OutOfMemoryError: Metaspace`, which matches the stale-generation/classloader retention. I’m patching the cache swap and worker-thread shutdown paths now so old frames unpin immediately on generation change and the final generation is released when the pool exits.

steps.message: Stress no longer appears to be the blocker; the remaining failure is the replay unload check, with both sessions reporting zero class unloads after close. That points to GC observability rather than a live-retention bug, so I’m checking the plugin loading model and the actual `java` launcher behavior before I decide whether to change lifecycle or reduce classloader churn.

steps.message: The `java` on this machine is a wrapper, and it is definitely injecting `-XX:+DisableExplicitGC` from the command line. That explains why the replay runner’s `System.gc()` never drives class unloading, even after the reachability leak is fixed. I’m looking at the wrapper only far enough to confirm the launch contract, then I’ll adjust the runtime so the suite can pass under that launcher without touching `bin/*` or the runners.

I’m checking whether the service itself ever calls explicit GC outside the replay path; if not, the cleanest end-to-end fix is to remove that launcher override now that the actual pin leak is fixed.

steps.message:
**Changes**

I fixed the generation-retention bug in the daemon lifecycle.

- In [ThreadCache.java](/workspace/textd/src/main/java/io/harbor/textd/core/ThreadCache.java:13), a worker now closes its previous `WorkerHandle` before switching to a new generation, so old frames are unpinned immediately instead of accumulating across reloads.
- In [TextDaemon.java](/workspace/textd/src/main/java/io/harbor/textd/core/TextDaemon.java:94), worker threads now call `ThreadBridge.clear()` on thread exit, which releases the final cached handle when a session closes so the last generation can drain and its plugin classloader can be reclaimed.
- In [/opt/textd/jvm.cfg](/opt/textd/jvm.cfg:1), I flipped the wrapped launcher from `-XX:+DisableExplicitGC` to `-XX:-DisableExplicitGC`. The replay validator depends on `System.gc()` to observe class unloading after close, and the wrapper was suppressing that.

The text-processing path and outputs were not changed.

**Validation**

`bin/run-suite` now passes end to end: `suite-ok`.

A direct replay run also shows unloading after both sessions close, with deterministic outputs unchanged:
- first session unload delta: `612`
- second session unload delta: `820`
